---
title: "Explore the knowledge graph"
subtitle: "The node & relationship schema, wired up with synthetic example data — click around"
author: "Veronika Palotai"
date: "2026-07-13"
categories: [knowledge-graph, kuzu, visualization]
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: false
---

The warehouse feeds a **Kuzu** property graph with a `Day` spine that stitches every
domain together — cycle phase, labs, nutrition, activity. Below is that exact schema
(11 node types, 12 relationship types), populated with a small **synthetic** example
day so you can see how a real record threads together. **Click any node** to highlight
its connections and inspect its (fake) properties; click the background to reset.

Follow the nutrition chain — `Meal —EATEN→ Food —HAS_NUTRIENT→ Nutrient`: a logged meal
resolves to its canonical USDA food (with the portion in grams), and each food to its
full nutrient profile. That's how the graph knows the *magnesium and iron in the salmon*
even though the food log only ever recorded calories and macros. (Every food links to the
same complete nutrient set — 6 shown here, all 16 in the real graph.)

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
Every value here is invented. No real measurements appear anywhere on this site — this
is the *structure*, not my data.
:::

```{=html}
<style>
  #graph-wrap { position: relative; width: 100%; height: 560px; border: 1px solid #e6e6e6;
                border-radius: 8px; background: #fbfbfb; overflow: hidden; }
  #cy { width: 100%; height: 100%; }
  #legend { position: absolute; top: 12px; left: 12px; background: rgba(255,255,255,.92);
            border: 1px solid #e6e6e6; border-radius: 6px; padding: 10px 12px; font-size: 12px;
            line-height: 1.7; z-index: 5; }
  #legend b { font-size: 11px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .04em; color: #666; }
  .lg { display: inline-block; width: 11px; height: 11px; border-radius: 3px; margin-right: 6px;
        vertical-align: -1px; }
  #panel { position: absolute; top: 12px; right: 12px; width: 240px; background: rgba(255,255,255,.96);
           border: 1px solid #e6e6e6; border-radius: 6px; padding: 12px 14px; font-size: 13px;
           z-index: 5; display: none; box-shadow: 0 2px 10px rgba(0,0,0,.06); }
  #panel .ptype { font-size: 11px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .04em; }
  #panel h3 { margin: 4px 0 8px; font-size: 16px; }
  #panel table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; }
  #panel td { padding: 2px 0; vertical-align: top; }
  #panel td.k { color: #888; padding-right: 8px; white-space: nowrap; }
  #reset { position: absolute; bottom: 12px; left: 12px; z-index: 5; font-size: 12px;
           background: #fff; border: 1px solid #ccc; border-radius: 5px; padding: 5px 10px;
           cursor: pointer; color: #333; }
  #hint { position: absolute; bottom: 12px; right: 14px; z-index: 5; font-size: 11px; color: #999; }
</style>

<div id="graph-wrap">
  <div id="cy"></div>
  <div id="legend">
    <b>Node domains</b><br>
    <span class="lg" style="background:#1a1a1a"></span>Day (spine)<br>
    <span class="lg" style="background:#0072B2"></span>Cycle<br>
    <span class="lg" style="background:#E69F00"></span>Labs<br>
    <span class="lg" style="background:#009E73"></span>Nutrition<br>
    <span class="lg" style="background:#CC79A7"></span>Activity
  </div>
  <div id="panel"></div>
  <button id="reset">Reset view</button>
  <span id="hint">click a node to label its links · drag to pan · scroll to zoom</span>
</div>

<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/cytoscape@3.30.2/dist/cytoscape.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function () {
  var C = { spine:"#1a1a1a", cycle:"#0072B2", labs:"#E69F00", nutrition:"#009E73", activity:"#CC79A7" };
  function n(id, label, type, domain, props) {
    return { data: { id:id, label:label, ntype:type, color:C[domain], props:props } };
  }
  function e(s, t, rel, extra) { return { data: { source:s, target:t, rel:rel + (extra?" · "+extra:"") } }; }

  var nodes = [
    // --- personal timeline (Day-centred) ---
    n("day","2025-01-14","Day","spine",{ "cycle day":"14", weekday:"Tuesday", "phase":"ovulation" }),
    n("phase","ovulation","CyclePhase","cycle",{ "fertility zone":"fertile", "confirmed by":"BBT shift + mucus peak" }),
    n("sym","Cervical mucus","Symptom","cycle",{ value:"egg-white", ordinal:"5 (peak fertility)" }),
    n("act","Morning run","Activity","activity",{ distance:"5.2 km", duration:"31 min", energy:"410 kcal", source:"Strava" }),
    n("meal_b","Breakfast","Meal","nutrition",{ logged:"08:10", items:"oat porridge, banana" }),
    n("meal_d","Dinner","Meal","nutrition",{ logged:"19:40", items:"salmon, greens" }),
    n("lab","Estradiol — lab result","LabResult","labs",{ value:"1108 pmol/L", date:"2025-01-14", "as-of status":"normal", "measured as":"Estradiol (biomarker)" }),
    n("bm","Estradiol (biomarker)","Biomarker","labs",{ category:"hormone", unit:"pmol/L", key:"estradiol", note:"canonical analyte — every Estradiol result links here via MEASURED_AS" }),
    n("rr","Estradiol — reference range","ReferenceRange","labs",{ interval:"46–607 pmol/L", "valid from":"2024-02", note:"judged as-of the result's date" }),
    n("food_o","Oatmeal, cooked","Food","nutrition",{ "fdc_id":"(synthetic)", note:"its full USDA nutrient profile is on the HAS_NUTRIENT edges" }),
    n("food_s","Salmon, cooked","Food","nutrition",{ "fdc_id":"(synthetic)", note:"its full USDA nutrient profile is on the HAS_NUTRIENT edges" }),
    n("ing_oats","Rolled oats","Ingredient","nutrition",{ "used in foods":"312" }),
    n("ing_water","Water","Ingredient","nutrition",{ "used in foods":"1,204" }),
    n("ing_salmon","Atlantic salmon","Ingredient","nutrition",{ "used in foods":"46" })
  ];
  var edges = [
    e("day","phase","IN_PHASE"),
    e("sym","day","OBSERVED_ON","egg-white"),
    e("act","day","PERFORMED_ON"),
    e("meal_b","day","LOGGED_ON"),
    e("meal_d","day","LOGGED_ON"),
    e("lab","day","RESULT_ON"),
    e("lab","bm","MEASURED_AS"),
    e("rr","bm","REF_FOR"),
    // the reconciled link — a logged meal → its canonical USDA food, portion in grams
    e("meal_b","food_o","EATEN","40 g"),
    e("meal_d","food_s","EATEN","150 g"),
    e("food_o","ing_oats","COMPOSED_OF","40 g"),
    e("food_o","ing_water","COMPOSED_OF","160 g"),
    e("food_s","ing_salmon","COMPOSED_OF","150 g")
  ];

  // The nutrient layer is generated from data, so it is symmetric by construction:
  // EVERY food links to the SAME full nutrient set (the real graph has all 16 per food).
  // [key, label, unit, oatmeal/100g, salmon/100g]; first four are the logged macros.
  var NUTR = [
    ["energy","Energy","kcal",71,206], ["protein","Protein","g",2.5,22],
    ["fat","Fat","g",1.5,12], ["carbohydrate","Carbohydrate","g",12,0.2],
    ["magnesium","Magnesium","mg",27,30], ["iron","Iron","mg",0.9,0.5]
  ];
  var MACROS = ["energy","protein","fat","carbohydrate"];
  NUTR.forEach(function (x) {
    var macro = MACROS.indexOf(x[0]) >= 0;
    nodes.push(n(x[0], x[1], "Nutrient", "nutrition",
      { type: macro ? "macronutrient (logged + USDA)" : "micronutrient (USDA only — never logged)",
        unit: x[2], amounts: "on the edges, not the node" }));
    edges.push(e("food_o", x[0], "HAS_NUTRIENT", x[3] + " " + x[2] + "/100g"));
    edges.push(e("food_s", x[0], "HAS_NUTRIENT", x[4] + " " + x[2] + "/100g"));
  });
  // logged macros only: per-meal CONTAINS + daily INTAKE_ON (Yazio logs the 4 macros)
  var MEAL_LOG = {
    meal_b: { energy:"210 kcal", protein:"6 g", fat:"4 g", carbohydrate:"30 g" },
    meal_d: { energy:"380 kcal", protein:"34 g", fat:"20 g", carbohydrate:"8 g" }
  };
  Object.keys(MEAL_LOG).forEach(function (mid) {
    MACROS.forEach(function (k) { edges.push(e(mid, k, "CONTAINS", MEAL_LOG[mid][k])); });
  });
  var DAY_TOTAL = { energy:"1650 kcal", protein:"92 g", fat:"70 g", carbohydrate:"150 g" };
  MACROS.forEach(function (k) { edges.push(e("day", k, "INTAKE_ON", DAY_TOTAL[k])); });

  var elements = nodes.concat(edges);

  var cy = cytoscape({
    container: document.getElementById("cy"),
    elements: elements,
    style: [
      { selector: "node", style: {
          "background-color": "data(color)", "label": "data(label)", "color": "#222",
          "font-size": 10, "text-wrap": "wrap", "text-max-width": 92, "text-valign": "bottom",
          "text-margin-y": 3, "width": 26, "height": 26, "border-width": 2, "border-color": "#fff" } },
      { selector: 'node[ntype = "Day"]', style: { "width": 46, "height": 46, "font-size": 12, "font-weight": "bold" } },
      { selector: "edge", style: {
          "font-size": 8, "color": "#555", "text-rotation": "autorotate",
          "text-background-color": "#fbfbfb", "text-background-opacity": 1, "text-background-padding": 2,
          "width": 1.4, "line-color": "#cdcdcd", "target-arrow-color": "#cdcdcd",
          "target-arrow-shape": "triangle", "arrow-scale": 0.9, "curve-style": "bezier" } },
      { selector: ".faded", style: { "opacity": 0.08, "text-opacity": 0 } },
      { selector: ".hi", style: { "border-color": "#333", "border-width": 3 } },
      // edge labels are shown only for the clicked node's edges — keeps the dense graph legible
      { selector: "edge.hi", style: { "label": "data(rel)", "line-color": "#333",
          "target-arrow-color": "#333", "width": 2.4, "color": "#222", "z-index": 99 } }
    ],
    layout: { name: "cose", idealEdgeLength: 68, nodeRepulsion: 16000, padding: 30, animate: true, animationDuration: 600, componentSpacing: 90 },
    wheelSensitivity: 0.2
  });
  // Guarantee the graph is framed on load (cose's own fit can race the container layout).
  cy.one("layoutstop", function () { cy.fit(undefined, 34); });

  var panel = document.getElementById("panel");
  function showPanel(node) {
    var d = node.data(), rows = "";
    for (var k in d.props) rows += '<tr><td class="k">' + k + '</td><td>' + d.props[k] + "</td></tr>";
    panel.innerHTML = '<div class="ptype" style="color:' + d.color + '">' + d.ntype +
      '</div><h3>' + d.label + "</h3><table>" + rows + "</table>";
    panel.style.display = "block";
  }
  cy.on("tap", "node", function (ev) {
    var node = ev.target, hood = node.closedNeighborhood();
    cy.elements().addClass("faded").removeClass("hi");
    hood.removeClass("faded").addClass("hi");
    showPanel(node);
  });
  cy.on("tap", function (ev) {
    if (ev.target === cy) { cy.elements().removeClass("faded hi"); panel.style.display = "none"; }
  });
  document.getElementById("reset").onclick = function () {
    cy.elements().removeClass("faded hi"); panel.style.display = "none";
    cy.animate({ fit: { padding: 30 } }, { duration: 400 });
  };
})();
</script>
```

## How to read it — the nutrition chain, and a few modelling notes

Everything hangs off the `Day` spine — cycle phase, symptoms, a lab result (judged
against the *reference range in effect on its date*), meals, daily nutrient totals, an
activity. That spine is what turns cross-domain questions — "average BBT by cycle
phase", "protein on active vs quiet days" — into a single graph traversal.

The **nutrition chain is the payoff**, and it used to be a gap. A logged `Meal` now links
to its canonical USDA `Food` via **`EATEN`** (with the portion in grams), and each `Food`
to its per-100g profile via **`HAS_NUTRIENT`**. Building that `Meal → Food` edge means
reconciling a named, cross-lingual log entry to the right USDA row — exactly the problem
the [food-retrieval study](writeup_food_retrieval.qmd) solves; its `food_map` output is
what populates `EATEN`. The reward: traverse `Meal —EATEN→ Food —HAS_NUTRIENT→ Nutrient`
and you can compute a nutrient that was **never logged** — a day's magnesium, the iron in
the salmon — as `Σ portion/100 × the food's per-100g amount`.

A couple of modelling choices worth calling out, since the picture invites the questions:

- **`Nutrient` is a shared *type* node, not a value.** A macro node like `Protein` looks
  "empty" because the quantity lives on the **edge** — `Breakfast —CONTAINS·6 g→ Protein`
  (the logged macro), `Salmon —HAS_NUTRIENT·22 g/100g→ Protein` (the USDA profile). One
  canonical node per nutrient is reused everywhere (like `Biomarker`).
- **The profile is systematic, not cherry-picked.** *Every* `Food` links to the *same*
  full nutrient set via `HAS_NUTRIENT` (6 here, all 16 in the real graph) — the edges are
  generated from the USDA data, so there are no arbitrary gaps.
- **Two nutrition signals, on purpose.** `CONTAINS`/`INTAKE_ON` carry the *logged* macros
  (what the app recorded — energy, protein, fat, carbs), while `HAS_NUTRIENT` carries the
  full *reference* profile from USDA. `Magnesium` and `Iron` connect only to the foods,
  not to the meal, because the log never recorded them — they're derived through the link.
- **`Estradiol` *is* connected to its result** — `LabResult —MEASURED_AS→ Biomarker`
  (and `ReferenceRange —REF_FOR→ Biomarker`). The three "Estradiol" nodes are a result,
  its analyte, and its reference range.
